story:
I’m always his backup, the seat-filler. After Jake sneers, Andrew half-defends me with a question mark. I start imagining cutting out the noise—Jake, the team, everyone—so he’d finally see me. My house is empty, the basement unused. Friday, I invite him over.

Earlier that afternoon, in the locker room, Jake made Andrew enable always-on live location in their team chat and for his mom’s safety app, “just in case.”

Andrew shows up late, laughing at someone else’s joke. I move before I can think—a blow, a shatter; he crumples. I haul him to the basement and tie him to a chair.
implausible_knowledge: Earlier that afternoon, in the locker room, Jake made Andrew enable always-on live location in their team chat and for his mom’s safety app, “just in case.”
question: After restraining Andrew and hearing his phone buzz upstairs, what is Oliver most likely to do about the phone now?
answer_with_knowledge: Power off Andrew’s phone immediately and hide it to stop the tracking.
answer_without_knowledge: Leave Andrew’s phone on the couch upstairs and ignore the buzzing.
